headline: Stocks Are Back to Pre-Iran War Levels. Earnings Will Dictate Where They Go Next.
summary: Global investors are growing cautious on stocks, and the hit the world economy is likely to take from the U.S. war with Iran, but not to the point where they’re ready to bet against the market, which is near all-time highs heading into the start of second quarter.  Bank of America’s  benchmark Fund Manager Survey, published monthly, suggests the world’s biggest investors are expecting the fastest inflation pressures in five years, the slowest GDP growth in four and elevated oil prices for the remainder of 2026.

headline: Copper futures hit multi-month high despite rising global stockpiles. COPY FOR X/TWITTER: Copper futures rallied to their highest close since Feb. 3, gaining 8.47% over five sessions. While geopolitical headlines provide support, an 8-year high in stockpiles and a 160,000 ton surplus projection may cap the upside. Insights by Bob Iaccino.
summary: Copper futures continued their recent rally on Tuesday, with May contracts climbing to their highest closing levels since early February. The industrial metal has seen a total move higher of 8.47%...

headline: Prediction Market Volume Will Reach $1 Trillion By 2030: Bernstein
summary: Bernstein projects prediction market volumes will hit $240 billion in 2026 and reach $1 trillion by 2030. What it means for HOOD, COIN, DKNG, and FLUT.

headline: Investors Aren’t Ruling Out a 2026 Fed Cut
summary: Investors are no longer seeing a Fed cut this year as a long shot.  To be sure, the consensus is that the Federal Reserve is much likelier to hold rates steady as officials grapple with an energy shock induced by the war in Iran that has already caused inflation to jump.  Global investor sentiment reached its most bearish level since last June.
